headline: SEBI settlement exposes urgent governance risk; board action needed to avoid ₹1630.2 Cr impact.
board_paragraph: YES Bank faces a material governance event following SEBI's ₹964.6 Cr settlement (engine estimate) over Yes Bank bonds, with an additional ₹665.6 Cr in indirect regulatory and reputational risk (engine estimate), totaling ₹1630.2 Cr. The board must approve a sector-leading governance reform plan within 4 weeks to mitigate enforcement escalation and defend ESG index inclusion. Inaction risks enforcement by SEBI, rating downgrades, and up to ₹1630.2 Cr in direct and indirect costs (engine estimate), with potential long-term investor and workforce attrition impacts.

stakeholder_map.stance: Enforcement escalation if remediation plan absent within 30-60 days. Formal show-cause → interim penalty → appeal window. Settlement via Consent Mechanism in 40-60% of cases.
Direct intervention via moratorium, AQR, or governance restructuring for material asset-quality or fraud events. Climate stress test framework applies to top-16 banks from FY27.

AGAINST vote recommendation on audit committee chair or CEO re-election for material governance failures. Downgrade of governance rating (MSCI/ISS QualityScore) by 1-2 bands. AGM season cadence: April-September.
stakeholder_map.precedent: Infosys 2017 Sikka exit → ISS flagged 2 AGMs

stakeholder_map.stance: Rating action (e.g., BBB → BB) within 90-180 days of material event. Watch-list status in interim. Flow-through to MSCI ESG Leaders / SRI indices triggers 1 (engine estimate)-3% AUM outflow from passive strategies.
stakeholder_map.precedent: Vedanta 2020 B→CCC → MSCI ESG Leaders exclusion

stakeholder_map.stakeholder: BlackRock + Norges Bank IM + CalPERS (Top-10 AUM)
stakeholder_map.stance: Private engagement first (letter/meeting) within 30-60 days. Vote against if remediation absent by next AGM. Exclusion from ESG mandate AUM if severity=high sustained 2+ quarters. Position review on 8-12% AUM flows.
stakeholder_map.precedent: NBIM 2017 excluded 11 coal miners incl. Adani Ports

stakeholder_map.stakeholder: Workforce (via attrition, unions, whistleblowers, review platforms)
stakeholder_map.stance: Attrition spike 5-15% in 6-12 months post-event; Glassdoor ratings drop 0.3-0.5; recruitment cost rises 20-40%. Union/works council engagement triggers NLRA-style litigation for covered entities. Whistleblower filings rise 2-4× after material event.

three_year_trajectory.do_nothing: If no remediation is undertaken, by FY27-29, YES Bank faces up to ₹1630.2 Cr in direct and indirect costs (engine estimate), likely credit rating downgrades, exclusion from ESG indices, and 1-3% AUM outflows. Investor confidence will erode, proxy advisors will recommend against key board members, and workforce attrition may spike, compounding reputational and financial damage.
three_year_trajectory.act_now: Implementing a sector-leading governance reform within 4 weeks at a cost within the ₹1630.2 Cr exposure (engine estimate) can restore trust, defend ESG index inclusion, and potentially reduce cost of capital by 20-40 bps (engine estimate). Expected benefits include rating stabilization, investor retention, and improved workforce morale, with recovery visible within 12-18 months.

qna_drafts.earnings_call: We acknowledge the SEBI settlement and are acting decisively to strengthen our governance framework. Our board has prioritized rapid implementation of sector-leading reforms to restore stakeholder trust and minimize long-term financial and reputational impact.
qna_drafts.press_statement: YES Bank has settled with SEBI and is committed to implementing robust governance reforms within the next four weeks. We are taking all necessary steps to address the issue, restore confidence, and ensure alignment with the highest regulatory and ESG standards.
qna_drafts.board_qa: Q: What is the maximum penalty exposure? A: The total direct and indirect exposure is ₹1630.2 Cr (engine estimate). Q: What is the FY26 capex impact? A: Capex plans may require reprioritization to accommodate governance reform costs within the ₹1630.2 Cr exposure (engine estimate). Q: How soon must we act? A: A remediation plan must be approved and communicated within 4 weeks to avoid enforcement escalation.
qna_drafts.regulator_qa: We are fully cooperating with SEBI and have initiated a comprehensive governance reform program. A detailed remediation plan will be submitted within the stipulated 30-60 day window, and we commit to ongoing engagement to ensure full compliance and timely resolution.

warnings: verifier: added 3 source tags on ₹ figures
